cards.name: Deck of Many Things
cards.rules_text:
The Deck of Many Things is a shared deck in the command zone.
DRAW AT YOUR OWN RISK!
Setup —
• Shuffle the Deck and place it in the command zone.
• Each player begins the game with 1 Fate Point (maximum 1).
Drawing —
Beginning on your fifth turn, once during your precombat main phase, you may spend a Fate Point to draw a card from the Deck.
Gaining Fate Points —
If you have no Fate Points, you may roll a d6 during your main phase. On a 6, you gain 1 Fate Point. If you already have one, you may draw a card from the Deck instead.
Resolution —
Cards drawn from the Deck are cast immediately without paying their mana cost. Drawing from the Deck can't be countered, doesn't use the stack, and can only be responded to by The Fates.
• Cards that would leave the battlefield are shuffled back into the Deck, except The Jester and The Fool, which are exiled.
• Resolve drawn cards one at a time in the order drawn.
• Emblems from the Deck can't be removed except by effects that specifically remove emblems.
• The legend rule doesn't apply to permanents from the Deck.
Fate Glimpsed Once —
A player can't cast the same card from the Deck more than once per game. If you would reveal a card you've already cast from the Deck, shuffle it back and reveal another.
The Deck Vanishes —
Once every player has cast at least one card from the Deck, exile the Deck. No further cards can be drawn from it this game.
Optional Rules —
• Each player may draw one card from the Deck at the start of the game without spending a Fate Point.
• When drawing, you may declare 1 to 4 cards instead of one. Resolve each in order.
cards.flavor_text: "Sometimes the cards you're dealt become the fate you're given."

cards.name: Game Master
cards.rules_text:
Game Master exists in a separate command zone as a pseudo-player. No life total. Can't win, lose, be targeted, or be dealt damage. Goes last in turn order.
Can be given control of permanents from the Deck of Many Things. Permanents under Game Master's control can be targeted normally.
("Each player" / "all players" / "opponents" exclude Game Master unless stated. If a creature Game Master controls doesn't specify a target, it attacks a player chosen at random.)
cards.flavor_text: "The deck has a hand. The hand has no face."

cards.name: FAQ
cards.rules_text:
Common questions and edge cases for the Deck of Many Things.
Format —
The Deck is a singleton format — only one copy of each card across the four sub-decks (Original, Expansion, Harrow, Wonder). Sub-decks may be played separately or combined.
Game Master Mode —
Optional fifth seat. The Game Master is a pseudo-player that controls permanents transferred from the Deck. Goes last in turn order, has no life total, can't be targeted, can't win or lose. See the Game Master rules card for specifics.
"Each player" / "each opponent" —
Excludes the Game Master unless explicitly stated. The GM is a pseudo-player, not a real one.
Targeting the GM —
The Game Master itself is not a valid target. Permanents under GM control can be targeted normally.
Drawing from the Deck —
Spend 1 Fate Point on your precombat main phase from turn 5 onward. The drawn card is cast free. Drawing can't be countered or responded to except by The Fates.
Legend rule —
Doesn't apply to permanents drawn from the Deck. Multiple legendary copies may coexist.
Reshuffling —
Deck permanents shuffle back when they leave the battlefield, except The Jester and The Fool, which are exiled.
One card per player per game —
A player can't cast the same Deck card more than once. If repeated, shuffle and re-reveal.
The Deck vanishes —
Once every player has drawn at least once, exile the Deck. No further draws.
Conflicting effects —
Resolve in the order cards were drawn. Multiple Deck emblems stack and apply simultaneously.
cards.flavor_text: "Read the card. The card is the rules. Then ask the table."

cards.name: Glossary
cards.rules_text:
Custom mechanics and statuses used across the Deck of Many Things.
Fate Points —
A resource each player begins with (maximum 1). Spend 1 to draw from the Deck. If you have none, roll a d6 in your main phase — on a 6, gain 1.
Eternal Damnation —
Permanent lockout from drawing further cards from the Deck. Existing emblems and permanents remain.
Incapacitated —
Emblem status (Soul Prison): can't gain life, attack with creatures, or activate non-mana abilities. May cast only one spell per turn.
Engulfed —
Status from the Gelatinous Cube: you are removed from active play until the Cube leaves the battlefield.
Marked —
Player-tracking status (Avatar of Death, The Demon, Vengeful Archfiend). A specified creature must attack the marked player each combat if able.
Dimensionally Displaced —
Status from the Donjon and Temporal Stasis Sphere: your body is incapacitated and you can't draw further cards. A wish spell or specific effect reveals your prison.
Deprivation Counters —
The Mute Hag inflicts these; each type restricts a sense:
• Sight: Can't scry, surveil, or search libraries. Spells and abilities you control can't target opponents' permanents.
• Hearing: Can't cast spells or activate abilities during other players' turns.
• Speech: Creatures you control can't attack or block.
Despair Counters —
The Soul Prison accumulates these each upkeep. Spells cost {1} more for each Despair Counter.
Vanishing —
A permanent enters with N time counters; remove one each upkeep. When the last is removed, the permanent's effect resolves and it is sacrificed.
Living Aura —
When this Aura enters, if you control no creatures, create a 0/0 Spirit token, then attach this to it.
Living Growth —
When this Aura enters, if you control no lands, create a basic land token of any type, then attach this Aura to it.
Wager —
Starting with you in turn order, offer each player the chance to wager. The first time a player declines, they discard a card, lose 5 life, and mill three cards. Each player may wager up to three times total; after their third wager, they must decline without triggering the penalty. Continue offering wagers in turn order until every player has declined consecutively since the last wager was placed, then the wager ends. To wager, choose a mode not yet chosen by you this wager and flip a coin: on a win, apply that mode's first listed effect; on a loss, its second.
Petrified —
You can't cast spells or activate abilities except those of the source. Your creatures have indestructible, enter tapped, don't untap, and can't attack. All damage dealt to you or your permanents is reduced to 1. You can't receive poison counters.
Restrained —
You can't cast more than one spell or activate more than one ability each turn. Artifacts and enchantments you control lose all abilities. Your creatures can't attack unless you pay {2} for each attacker.
Curse counters —
Permanents with curse counters have "At the beginning of your upkeep, this permanent deals 3 damage to you" and "Activated abilities of this permanent cost {3} more to activate."
Key counters —
Permanents with key counters have "{T}: Add one mana of any color."
Blessing counters —
Permanents with blessing counters have hexproof, vigilance, and "At the beginning of your upkeep, create a 1/1 white and green Spirit token with 'Whenever this attacks, put a blessing counter on another target permanent you control.'"
Treachery token —
An enchantment — Aura Curse token with "Enchant player," "At the beginning of enchanted player's upkeep, that player loses 1 life," and Vanishing 3.
Forge token —
An artifact token with "{T}, Sacrifice this artifact: Create a token that's a copy of target nontoken artifact you control, except it's also a 2/2 red and white Construct artifact creature with haste."
Ember token —
An artifact token with "{T}, Sacrifice this artifact: Add {R}{R}."
Artificer token —
A 3/1 red and white Artificer artifact creature token with haste and "When this creature dies, attach target Equipment you control to target creature you control."
Soul Gem token —
An artifact token with "When this artifact enters, it gains a charge counter for each point of power the linked creature had," "Remove a charge counter: Add one mana of any color," and "{B}, Pay X life, Sacrifice this artifact: Return the linked creature to the battlefield under its owner's control, where X is the greater of 1 and that creature's mana value."
Tavern token —
An artifact token with "At the beginning of each player's upkeep, that player may pay {1} and 1 life. If they do, they draw a card and create a 1/1 white and black Human Patron token."

• Search your library for a land and put it onto the battlefield tapped. Return it to your hand at the next end step unless you remove a portal counter.
• Exile another target permanent. Return it under its owner's control at the next end step unless you remove a portal counter.
• Put a permanent card with mana value 6 or less from your hand onto the battlefield. Return it to your hand at the next end step unless you remove a portal counter.
• Create a token copy of target permanent. It gains "When The Door leaves, sacrifice this" unless you remove a portal counter.
When The Door has no portal counters, sacrifice it.
cards.flavor_text: Eight passages. Eight possibilities. Choose wisely -- each one closes behind you.

cards.name: The Dragon
cards.rules_text:
Flying, menace
The Dragon gains abilities based on colors of other Dragons you control:
• White: Vigilance, protection from black. Other Dragons get +2/+2 and vigilance.
• Blue: Ward {3}, hexproof. Other Dragons have ward {3} and can't be countered.
• Black: Deathtouch, lifelink. Other Dragons have deathtouch and "Whenever this deals damage, each opponent loses 1 life."
• Red: Double strike. Other Dragons have first strike and "When this attacks, it deals 2 damage to any target."
• Green: Trample, reach. Other Dragons get +3/+1 and trample.
Whenever The Dragon attacks, create two 3/3 Dragon tokens with flying of a random color.
Growth — {T}, Sacrifice a Dragon: Put three +1/+1 counters on The Dragon and draw a card.
cards.flavor_text: Fate is not a line, but a tapestry—and I am every thread.

cards.name: The Elemental
cards.rules_text:
When The Elemental enters, choose a color.
You and permanents you control have protection from the chosen color.
{T}: Add three mana of the chosen color.
Spells you cast of the chosen color cost {1} less.
Whenever you cast a spell of the chosen color, you may change the chosen color.
cards.flavor_text: Choose your element. Become its vessel. Let all else break against you.

cards.name: The Expert
cards.rules_text:
Creatures you control have bushido 2X, and if damage would be dealt to you or a creature you control, prevent 2X of it, where X is The Expert's level.
Creatures you control have first strike.
Whenever one or more creatures you control deal combat damage to a player, draw a card.
Level 2 — {2}: Dexterity
Creatures you control have double strike and haste.
Whenever a creature you control deals combat damage to a player, you may have it deal 1 damage to target creature or planeswalker that player controls.
Level 3 — {3}: Dexterity
Creature spells you cast have flash. Creatures you control have flying.
Creature cards in your hand have ninjitsu {1}.
Level 4 — {4}: Dexterity
Creatures you control have skulk and hexproof.
At the beginning of combat on your turn, untap all creatures you control.
Whenever a creature you control deals combat damage to a creature, you may have it deal that much damage to another target creature that creature's controller controls.

cards.name: The Fey
cards.rules_text:
Shroud, vanishing 10
Shimmer — When The Fey enters, exile your hand and library. Create X 0/1 green and blue Faerie tokens with flying, where X is the number of cards exiled this way.
If you would draw a card while your library is empty, skip that draw instead.
Whenever a Faerie you control leaves, look at three random cards exiled with The Fey. You may cast one of them this turn without paying its mana cost.
{T}, Sacrifice a Faerie: Exile target nonland permanent. Its controller reveals cards from the top of their library until they reveal a nonland permanent and puts it onto the battlefield, then puts the rest on the bottom in a random order.
When The Fey leaves, shuffle all cards exiled with it into their owner's library.
cards.flavor_text: Time bends like a willow in the Feywild, and every moment is a gamble with eternity.

cards.name: The Fiend
cards.rules_text:
Flash, flying
When The Fiend enters, begin the wager. Modes (win / lose):
• Power — Put six +1/+1 counters on each creature you control / Sacrifice six creatures.
• Knowledge — Draw six cards / Mill six cards.
• Wealth — Search your library for six lands, put them onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle / Sacrifice six lands.
• Vengeance — Target player sacrifices six permanents / You sacrifice six permanents.
• Immortality — Gain 66 life / Your life total becomes 6.
• Dominion — Gain control of six permanents target player controls / Target opponent gains control of six permanents you control.
When The Fiend becomes the target of a spell or ability, is dealt damage, or the wager ends, shuffle it into the Deck.
cards.flavor_text: Pick your poison carefully, mortal. The greater the reward, the greater the fall.

cards.name: The Giant
cards.rules_text:
When The Giant enters, you gain 20 life.
Each creature you control with base power less than 10 has base power 10. Each creature you control with base toughness less than 10 has base toughness 10.
Each creature you control has trample, reach, and "This creature can block up to three creatures each combat. If it's blocking and would deal combat damage to an attacking creature in excess of that creature's toughness, the excess is dealt to that creature's controller instead."
If a source an opponent controls would deal damage to you or a permanent you control, prevent 1 of that damage.
cards.flavor_text: "The good news is we can see the objective. The bad news is it can reach us too." —Scout's last report

cards.name: The Humanoid
cards.rules_text:
Create a legendary 5/5 white Human Soldier token named Loyal Companion with vigilance, lifelink, indestructible, "Whenever you're dealt damage by a source an opponent controls, this creature deals that much damage to that source's controller," and "When this creature dies, you may draw a card from the Deck of Many Things."
When you lose the game, exile it.
cards.flavor_text: It breathed its first breath when you breathed your last prayer.

cards.name: The Juggernaut
cards.rules_text:
Creatures you control get +0/+X and ward X, where X is The Juggernaut's level.
Whenever one or more creatures you control are dealt damage and survive, draw a card.
Level 2 — {2}: Constitution
Creatures you control have persist and enter with a +1/+1 counter.
Whenever a creature you control is dealt damage, put a +1/+1 counter on it.
Your opponents can't cause you to sacrifice permanents.
Level 3 — {3}: Constitution
If one or more +1/+1 counters would be put on a creature you control, that many plus one are put on it instead.
Each creature you control assigns combat damage equal to its toughness rather than its power.
Level 4 — {4}: Constitution
Creatures you control have indestructible.
Each creature you control can block an additional creature each combat, and one blocking alone can block a creature with menace.
Whenever a source deals damage to a creature you control, that creature deals that much damage to that source.

cards.name: The Lance
cards.rules_text:
When The Lance enters, put a +1/+1 counter on each creature you control.
Whenever a creature you control attacks, put a +1/+1 counter on it.
At the beginning of your upkeep, put X +1/+1 counters distributed among any number of creatures you control, draw X cards, and create an X/X white Champion token with exalted, where X is The Lance's level.
Level 2 — {2}: Ascension
Creatures you control have evolve, mentor, and enter with a shield counter.
Whenever one or more +1/+1 counters are put on creatures you control, proliferate.
Level 3 — {3}: Ascension
Whenever you attack, double the number of +1/+1 counters on each attacking creature.
At the beginning of your end step, proliferate.
Level 4 — {4}: Ascension
Whenever you attack for the first time each turn, untap all creatures you control. After this phase, there is an additional combat phase.
The first time you proliferate each turn, proliferate an additional time.

cards.name: The Mage
cards.rules_text:
At the beginning of your upkeep, scry X, then draw a card. The first spell you cast each turn costs {X} less, where X is The Mage's level.
Level 2 — {2}: Intelligence
Spells you cast can't be countered. Creatures you control have prowess. Instant and sorcery spells you cast have rebound.
The first instant or sorcery you cast each turn has "When you cast this spell, copy it. You may choose new targets for the copy."
Level 3 — {3}: Intelligence
Spells you cast have cascade and delve.
You may cast instant and sorcery spells from your graveyard or from exile.
Level 4 — {4}: Intelligence
Spells you cast have storm.
Whenever you cast an instant or sorcery, copy it. You may choose new targets for the copy.
You may cast spells as though they had flash.

cards.name: The Map
cards.rules_text:
When The Map enters, put three seek counters on it.
{T}, Remove a seek counter: Choose one —
• Locate — Name a card. Reveal cards from the top of target player's library until you reveal it. Put it into your hand and shuffle the rest into that library. That player loses life equal to its mana value.
• Plunder — Look at target player's hand and choose a nonland card. That player discards it. You may cast it this turn, spending mana as though it were any color. If you don't, exile it.
• Cartograph — Search your library for an artifact, enchantment, or planeswalker card with mana value 4 or less and put it onto the battlefield. If it's a planeswalker, it enters with an additional loyalty counter, then shuffle.
cards.flavor_text: X marks the spot. Every spot.

cards.name: Curse of Exhaustion
cards.rules_text:
Enchant player
When Curse of Exhaustion enters, roll a d3. Put that many exhaustion counters on enchanted player.
1+ — Skip your draw step unless you discard a card.
2+ — Creatures you control get -2/-2.
3+ — Spells and nonmana activated abilities you play cost {1} more.
4 — When this counter is added, you lose half your life, rounded up.
5+ — Creatures you control can't attack or block.
6 — When this counter is added, you lose the game.
At the beginning of your end step, if you cast a spell or activated a nonmana ability this turn, put an exhaustion counter on you. Otherwise, remove one. If you have no exhaustion counters, sacrifice this curse.
cards.flavor_text: She stopped to rest, just for a moment. She never moved again.

cards.name: The Mine
cards.rules_text:
When The Mine enters, roll 2d6 (gems), then roll 1d10 (ore). Create X Treasure tokens, where X is (gems x 5) plus (ore x 3).
Excavate — At the beginning of your upkeep, roll a d6. Create that many Treasure tokens.
Prospect — {T}, Sacrifice The Mine: Roll 2d6. Create twice that many Treasure tokens.
cards.flavor_text: Some veins run deeper than the earth itself.

cards.name: The Monstrosity
cards.rules_text:
When this creature enters, roll a d20. Its base power and toughness each become that result plus 5. A random opponent gains control of it.
Haste, trample, menace
This creature can't block, attacks each combat if able, and can't attack any player other than the player who drew it.
When the player who drew it has 0 or less life or leaves the game, shuffle it into the Deck.
cards.flavor_text: Some horrors need no explanation—only survival.

cards.name: Gelatinous Cube
cards.rules_text:
Trample, menace, protection from instants and sorceries
When Gelatinous Cube enters, exile target creature you control; you become engulfed.
It gets +2/+2 for each engulfed player and attacks each combat if able.
Whenever it deals combat damage to a player, that player becomes engulfed.
Whenever it deals combat damage to a creature, exile that creature, then put a +1/+1 counter on Gelatinous Cube for each creature exiled with it.
When Gelatinous Cube leaves, all players stop being engulfed and all creatures exiled with it return to the battlefield.
cards.flavor_text: Its translucent form belies a hunger older than stone.

cards.name: The Path
cards.rules_text:
Creatures you control have haste.
{T}: Add one mana of any color.
Whenever a land enters tapped under your control, untap it.
You may play an additional land on each of your turns.
Landfall — Draw a card. This ability triggers only once each turn.
cards.flavor_text: The road unrolled before her like a welcome mat for the multiverse.

cards.name: The Pit
cards.rules_text:
Roll 3d6 and lose three times the result in life.
Fall — The Pit deals damage to each nonflying creature you control equal to half the life you lost this way, rounded down. Creatures that would die this way are exiled instead.
If this would reduce your life total to 0 or less, instead it becomes 1 and you skip your next two turns.
cards.flavor_text: The ground beneath your feet was never as solid as you believed.

cards.name: The Plant
cards.rules_text:
When The Plant enters, search your library for a land and put it onto the battlefield tapped. Create a 0/1 green Plant token for each land you control.
{T}: Add {G}{G}{G}.
Landfall — Whenever a land you control enters, put a +1/+1 counter on each Plant you control.
{1}: The Plant doesn't untap during its controller's next untap step. Choose one —
• Overgrowth — Create a 0/1 green Plant token for each land you control.
• Verdant — Until end of turn, lands you control become 2/2 Forest creatures that are still lands with "Whenever this deals combat damage to a player, create a 0/1 green Plant token."
• Entangle — Target land becomes a 0/4 Plant creature with defender and "{T}: Tap or untap target creature." It's still a land.
cards.flavor_text: First a seed. Then a sapling. Then a forest that remembers your name.

cards.name: The Priest
cards.rules_text:
At the beginning of your upkeep, look at the top X cards of target opponent's library, where X is The Priest's level. You may put any number of them on the bottom in any order. Gain X life.
At the beginning of your end step, if you gained life this turn, draw a card.
Level 2 — {2}: Wisdom
Creatures you control have lifelink and vigilance.
Whenever you gain life, put a +1/+1 counter on target creature you control, then surveil 1.
Level 3 — {3}: Wisdom
Whenever a creature enters, you gain 1 life.
Whenever you gain life, you may pay {1}. If you do, create a 1/1 white Spirit token with lifelink.
Level 4 — {4}: Wisdom
At the beginning of your end step, return target creature card with mana value X or less from any graveyard to the battlefield under your control, where X is the life you gained this turn.
Whenever a creature enters under your control, you gain life equal to its toughness.
You have hexproof.

cards.name: Curse of Restraint
cards.rules_text:
Enchant player, shroud
When Curse of Restraint enters, roll a d6 and add 2. Put that many bind counters on it. You become restrained, and spells you cast cost {1} more for each bind counter on it.
At the beginning of your upkeep, you lose life equal to the number of bind counters on it.
Whenever a creature deals combat damage to you or a creature you control, it deals 2 additional damage.
{0}, Pay X life and exile X cards from your hand or X nontoken permanents you control: Remove X bind counters. X can't be greater than 2. Activate only as a sorcery.
When Curse of Restraint has no bind counters, sacrifice it.
Eternal Damnation
cards.flavor_text: The chains tightened with every thought of escape.

cards.name: The Ring
cards.rules_text:
Indestructible
When The Ring enters, roll a d12 and add 4. Put that many charge counters on it. You gain protection from everything until your next turn.
At the beginning of your upkeep, put two charge counters on it.
At the beginning of your end step, you lose 1 life for each charge counter on it.
{T}, Remove X charge counters: Choose one —
• Empower — Until end of turn, creatures you control get +X/+X and gain your choice of flying, vigilance, or trample.
• Burden — Draw X cards and lose X life.
• Corrupt — Target player loses X life and you gain X life. X can't be greater than 10.
cards.flavor_text: Some gifts are too powerful to resist, too precious to relinquish.

cards.name: The Shield
cards.rules_text:
Indestructible
Living weapon
Bulwark — At the beginning of your upkeep or whenever equipped creature blocks or becomes blocked, you may put a fortification counter on it.
Equipped creature gets +1/+3 for each fortification counter on The Shield.
Transmutation — {0}: Remove all abilities The Shield granted to equipped creature, then choose two — indestructible, hexproof, shroud, protection from a color of your choice, ward {6}, absorb 3, reach, vigilance, defender, persist, undying, lifelink, or regenerate. It gains them until end of turn.
Equip {0}
cards.flavor_text: Those who seek to break it teach it only new ways to be unbreakable.

cards.name: The Ship
cards.rules_text:
At the beginning of your upkeep, create a 2/2 blue, black, and red Pirate token with "When this enters, if you control three or more Pirates, search your library for a land, put it onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle."
Whenever a Pirate you control attacks, it gains your choice of first strike, deathtouch, lifelink, or vigilance until end of turn.
Whenever a Pirate you control deals combat damage to a player, draw a card and create a Treasure token.
Crew 8
Whenever The Ship becomes crewed, choose three — flying, trample, lifelink, double strike, menace, hexproof, or annihilator 1. It gains those abilities until end of turn.
cards.flavor_text: Part of the crew, part of the ship.

cards.name: The Staff
cards.rules_text:
At the beginning of your upkeep, put a dominion counter on The Staff.
{T}, Choose one. If it has three or more dominion counters, you may choose any number instead:
• Add three mana of any one color.
• Draw two cards, then discard a card.
• Target player loses 3 life and you gain 3 life.
• The Staff deals 3 damage to any target.
• Create a 3/3 blue Elemental token with flying.
• Return target card with mana value 3 or less from your graveyard to your hand.
• Counter target spell or ability.
• Exile target nonland permanent until The Staff leaves.
cards.flavor_text: In its crystalline core, reality itself bends to your will.

cards.name: The Stairway
cards.rules_text:
Choose one —
• Compel — Target player draws a card from the Deck.
• Dictate — The next time a player would draw from the Deck this game, they draw two cards instead and you choose which resolves first. Exile the other.
• Deny — The next time target player would draw from the Deck this game, they skip that draw instead.
• Wonder — Search your library for an artifact card, put it onto the battlefield with indestructible, then shuffle.
cards.flavor_text: Some stairs lead up. Some lead down. These lead everywhere at once.

cards.name: Curse of Petrification
cards.rules_text:
Enchant player, shroud
When Curse of Petrification enters, you become petrified, but may still activate its abilities.
Whenever you're dealt damage, put an erosion counter on it.
{1}, Pay X life or sacrifice X nontoken, nonland permanents: Put X erosion counters on it. X can't be greater than 3. Activate only as a sorcery.
When Curse of Petrification has seven or more erosion counters, sacrifice it.
cards.flavor_text: In stillness, eternity. In silence, preservation. In stone, waiting.

cards.name: The Tavern
cards.rules_text:
At the beginning of your upkeep, create X 0/1 black and white Follower tokens with battle cry, where X is The Tavern's level.
Creatures you control have training.
Level 2 — {2}: Charisma
Creatures you control have "Whenever this creature becomes trained, draw a card" and "Tap two untapped creatures you control: Tap target creature an opponent controls."
Creature spells you cast have convoke.
Level 3 — {3}: Charisma
At the beginning of your upkeep, gain control of target creature an opponent controls with power X or less until end of turn, where X is the number of creatures you control. It gains haste.
Whenever a creature enters under your control, put a +1/+1 counter on it.
Level 4 — {4}: Charisma
Whenever a noncommander creature enters under an opponent's control, you may pay {2}. If you do, gain control of that creature.
Creatures you control get +1/+1 for each different creature type among Cleric, Rogue, Warrior, and Wizard you control.

cards.name: Divine Intervention
cards.rules_text:
When Divine Intervention enters, exile it. You may cast it from exile without paying its mana cost any time you could cast an instant. If you do, choose a mode you haven't chosen this game:
• Return all permanent cards from your graveyard to the battlefield.
• Your life total becomes your starting life total.
• Exile all nonland permanents your opponents control.
• Search your library for up to five cards and put them into your hand.
If you would lose the game while it's exiled with one or more intervention counters on it, you may cast it instead. If you do, your life total becomes your starting life total, remove all poison counters from you, reset all commander damage dealt to you, and return target creature from your graveyard to the battlefield tapped.
When cast from exile, shuffle it into the Deck.
Divine Intervention enters with two intervention counters. When you cast it from exile, remove one. When the last is removed, exile it permanently.
cards.flavor_text: Pray not for salvation. Pray for timing.

cards.name: True Resurrection
cards.rules_text:
When True Resurrection enters, exile it.
While it's exiled, if you would lose the game, instead your life total becomes your starting life total, remove all poison counters, curses, and -1/-1 counters from you and permanents you control, and return all creature cards from your graveyard to your hand. You skip your next draw step, then shuffle it into the Deck.
You may cast it from exile without paying its mana cost any time you could cast an instant. If you do, return target creature card from any graveyard to the battlefield under its owner's control with indestructible until end of turn. Restore all Auras and Equipment attached to it when it died, then shuffle it into the Deck.
cards.flavor_text: The grave opened. The light poured in. She blinked.

cards.name: The Tower
cards.rules_text:
Collapse — Destroy all artifacts you control. Sacrifice all lands you control. You can’t play lands during your next two turns.
You may draw a card from the Deck of Many Things.
cards.flavor_text: Everything you built. Everything you owned. Rubble.

cards.name: The Tree
cards.rules_text:
Enchant land
Living Growth
When The Tree enters, draw three cards and gain 8 life.
Enchanted land is a 4/8 green Plant creature with "At the beginning of your upkeep, put two +1/+1 counters on this and create a 0/1 green Plant token with reach and defender." It's still a land.
Other Plants you control get +1/+1 for each counter on enchanted creature.
When The Tree leaves, create X 1/1 green Plant tokens with reach and defender, where X is enchanted creature's toughness, and create a basic Forest land token.
cards.flavor_text: Some seek to escape death. Others choose to become life itself.

cards.name: Vengeful Revenant
cards.rules_text:
Vanishing 10
Haste, flying, shadow
Vengeful Revenant's power and toughness are each equal to 2 plus the number of creature cards in all graveyards.
When Vengeful Revenant enters, if no player is haunted by it, the player who drew it becomes haunted and gets an emblem with "At the beginning of your upkeep, sacrifice a creature. If you can't, you lose 1 life." A random opponent gains control of Vengeful Revenant.
It can't block and attacks the haunted player each combat if able.
If it would leave the battlefield, instead return it at the beginning of the next end step. This doesn't apply if it's being shuffled into the Deck.
Any player may pay life equal to its power: Shuffle it into the Deck. Activate only if it's on the battlefield.
cards.flavor_text: Born from the slaughter of an entire army, it bears the wrath of a hundred souls.

cards.name: The Warrior
cards.rules_text:
Creatures you control get +X/+X, frenzy X, and rampage X, where X is The Warrior's level.
Whenever one or more creatures you control fight, block, or become blocked, draw a card.
Level 2 — {2}: Strength
Creatures you control have trample and riot.
At the beginning of combat on your turn, target creature you control may fight target creature an opponent controls.
Level 3 — {3}: Strength
Creatures you control have menace and deathtouch.
Whenever a creature you control deals combat damage to a player, put two +1/+1 counters on it and amass Orcs 2.
Level 4 — {4}: Strength
Creatures you control have reach and can't be prevented from dealing combat damage.
If a creature you control would deal combat damage, it deals double that damage instead.
Whenever you attack, target defending player sacrifices a creature.

cards.name: Well of Minor Magics
cards.rules_text:
When Well of Minor Magics enters, choose three. You gain those abilities as long as you control it.
• At the beginning of your upkeep, scry 1.
• {T}: Add one mana of any color.
• {0}: Target creature you control gains haste until end of turn. Activate only once each turn.
• Whenever you cast a spell, you may pay {1}. If you do, it deals 1 damage to any target.
• At the beginning of your end step, draw a card.
cards.flavor_text: Each ripple in its depths whispers secrets of a different art.

cards.name: The Avalanche
cards.rules_text:
Split Second
Each player sacrifices all but one land they control.
All creatures get -8/-8 until end of turn and can't block this turn. If a creature would die this turn, exile it instead. At the beginning of the next end step, for each creature exiled this way, create a 0/1 colorless Wall token with defender and "When this creature dies, each player loses 1 life."
cards.flavor_text: "The earth doesn't care if you survive. The void beneath it hopes you don't."

cards.name: The Bear
cards.rules_text:
Trample, ward {2}
When this creature enters, put X +1/+1 counters on it, where X is your devotion to green.
Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player or permanent, double the number of +1/+1 counters on it. Each other player loses 2 life and discards a card unless they sacrifice a permanent.
Pay 2 life: Put a +1/+1 counter on this creature. Activate only during your turn.
cards.flavor_text: The curse took his form. The blessing took everything else.

cards.name: Curse of Vulnerability
cards.rules_text:
Enchant player
Vanishing 5
Whenever a creature enchanted player controls blocks or becomes blocked by a creature an opponent controls, the creature enchanted player controls gets -2/-2 until end of combat.
If damage would be dealt to enchanted player or creatures they control by a source an opponent controls, that source deals double that damage instead.
cards.flavor_text: "In the end, it wasn't the pain that broke him. It was knowing there would always be more."

cards.name: The Betrayal
cards.rules_text:
Choose target opponent. Gain control of all creatures that player controls until end of turn and they gain haste. For each creature that changed control this way, create a Treachery token attached to that player.
Whenever a creature you control but don't own deals combat damage to a player this turn, exile the top card of that player's library. You may play it this turn without paying its mana cost.
cards.flavor_text: "They fought beside you yesterday. They'll fight against you now." -- Kaya, the Betrayer

cards.name: The Big Sky
cards.rules_text: You get an emblem with "Once during each of your turns, you may have target creature you control get +10/+10 and gain trample until end of turn. When that creature deals combat damage this turn, destroy target artifact."
cards.flavor_text: When the sky opens, even mountains bow.

cards.name: The Brass Dwarf
cards.rules_text:
When this creature enters, create three Forge tokens.
Artifacts you control have "{T}: Add {R}."
Whenever you sacrifice an artifact, this creature deals 2 damage to any target. If the sacrificed artifact was a token, create a Treasure token.
cards.flavor_text: "In my forge, even scrap metal dreams of becoming masterworks."

cards.name: The Carnival
cards.rules_text:
When you cast this spell, exile the top nine cards of the Deck. An opponent looks at those cards, then shuffles them face down. Choose one at random and cast it without paying its mana cost, targeting you where legal; otherwise you choose its targets. Shuffle the remaining exiled cards into the Deck.
Flip a coin. If you win, repeat this process; if you lose, discard a card at random.
cards.flavor_text: "Behind these curtains lie nine fates. Choose carefully — or don't. The show must go on."

cards.name: The Courtesan
cards.rules_text:
When this creature enters, target artifact you control becomes an artifact creature with base power and toughness equal to its mana value, is also a Construct, has indestructible, and gains two of the following at random:
• Flying and vigilance
• Deathtouch and lifelink
• Double strike
• Hexproof and ward {2}
• "Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, draw two cards."
• "At the beginning of your upkeep, create a Treasure token."
If you control no artifacts, instead search your library for an artifact card, put it onto the battlefield as a 4/4 Construct artifact creature with two of these at random, then shuffle.
The chosen artifact stays a creature after this creature leaves.
cards.flavor_text: She spoke to the sword, and the sword answered.

cards.name: The Cricket
cards.rules_text:
When this creature enters, you may draw up to three additional cards from the Deck of Many Things, putting a Cricket counter on it for each.
At the beginning of your upkeep, you may reveal the top X cards of your library, where X is its Cricket counters. Put any number of lands revealed this way onto the battlefield tapped and the rest on the bottom in a random order.
Landfall — Draw X cards, where X is its Cricket counters. This triggers only twice each turn.
You may play an additional land on each of your turns for each Cricket counter on it.
cards.flavor_text: "Curiosity often leaps faster than caution."

sim_effect.label: The Cricket sings — draw up to 3 more from the Deck?

cards.name: The Crows
cards.rules_text: Each player chooses a creature they control and, if able, an artifact or enchantment they control. A random opponent chooses one of those permanents for each player, and that player sacrifices it. For each permanent sacrificed this way, its controller's opponents each draw a card. Each player who controls no artifact or enchantment instead loses 3 life and discards a card.
cards.flavor_text: "The crows care not for gold or flesh — only that a price is paid."

cards.name: Elder Air Elemental
cards.rules_text:
Vanishing 4
Flash, flying, haste, ward {2}
When this creature enters, mark you, then a random opponent gains control of it.
When the last time counter is removed, exile all the marked player's nonland permanents. That player planeswalks to the Plane of Air.
This creature attacks the marked player each combat if able and can't block. While it's on the battlefield, other creatures and players can't attack or target them.
cards.flavor_text: "Face me alone, mortal. Time grows short, and the winds of fate grow restless."

cards.name: The Dance
cards.rules_text:
Initiative — If you would roll a die or flip a coin, instead roll two dice or flip two coins and choose one of those results.
You may cast spells as though they had flash.
Whenever you cast a spell during another player's turn, draw a card.
cards.flavor_text: "To master chance is to learn its rhythm, to feel its pulse, to know its steps."

cards.name: The Demon's Lantern
cards.rules_text:
At the beginning of each opponent's upkeep, that player loses 1 life unless they pay {B}.
{1}: Choose target creature an opponent controls. Its controller may pay {1}. If they don't, exile it and create a Soul Gem artifact token linked to that creature.
If this permanent leaves, destroy all Soul Gems.
cards.flavor_text: "The gem's light glows brighter with every whisper of despair."

cards.name: The Desert
cards.rules_text:
Vanishing 3
When this permanent enters, search your library for up to three land cards, put them onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle.
You may exile any number of permanents you control and up to three creatures other players control. When this permanent leaves, return all cards exiled with it to the battlefield under their owners' control.
cards.flavor_text: One step across burning sand. The next, a thousand leagues away.

cards.name: The Demon
cards.rules_text:
Flying, haste
When this creature enters, choose a player. That player becomes marked, and their maximum hand size is reduced by this creature's doom counters.
Whenever this creature deals combat damage to the marked player, put a doom counter on it and scry 1.
At the beginning of the marked player's upkeep, this creature deals them damage equal to the number of doom counters on it.
Whenever a doom counter is put on this creature, if it has five or more, the marked player loses the game.
cards.flavor_text: "Your soul caught my eye. Soon, it shall catch my claws."

cards.name: The Foreign Trader
cards.rules_text:
Flash, deathtouch
When this creature enters, you may pay any amount of life in multiples of 5. For each 5 life paid, create three Treasure tokens, put an age counter on it, and scry 1. Otherwise, sacrifice it and create a Treasure token.
At the beginning of your upkeep, mill three cards for each age counter on this creature.
Your maximum hand size is increased, and spells you cast cost {1} less, for each age counter on this creature.
Whenever you sacrifice a Treasure token, each opponent loses 1 life and you gain 1 life.
cards.flavor_text: "Time is the only currency that cannot be counterfeited. Shall we trade?"

cards.name: The Forge
cards.rules_text:
When this permanent enters, create three Ember tokens.
{T}, Sacrifice an Equipment you control: Search your library for an Equipment with equal or lesser mana value and put it onto the battlefield attached to a creature you control with "Equipped creature gets +4/+4." Its equip ability can't be activated, and it stays attached to that creature as long as that creature remains on the battlefield. Then create an Ember token and shuffle.
Whenever an Equipment enters under your control, create a 3/1 red and white Artificer token.
Whenever you sacrifice an artifact, this permanent deals 2 damage to any target, plus 2 more if it was a token.
cards.flavor_text: She fed the forge a dagger. It returned a destiny.

cards.name: The Eclipse
cards.rules_text:
When this permanent enters, each player sacrifices all creatures they control, then loses 1 life for each creature sacrificed this way.
Whenever a player loses life, put that many tribute counters on this permanent. When it has forty or more, sacrifice it.
At the beginning of each player's upkeep, that player sacrifices a nonland permanent, then creates a 3/1 red Horror token with "When this leaves, it deals 3 damage to you." If they can't sacrifice, they lose 3 life and discard a card instead.
Creatures have "At the beginning of your end step, exile this creature unless you pay life equal to its power."
cards.flavor_text: "All things end. The Eclipse demands tribute."

cards.name: The Empty Throne
cards.rules_text:
When The Empty Throne enters, create five Treasure tokens.
{T}, choose one —
• Create two Treasure tokens and gain 2 life.
• Create two 1/1 white and black Noble tokens with "Other creatures you control get +1/+0."
• Nobles you control gain flying and vigilance until end of your next turn.
• Each opponent loses life equal to the number of Nobles you control.
Nobles you control have "Whenever this creature attacks, create a Treasure token."
cards.flavor_text: The throne sat empty for a thousand years. It was waiting for you.

cards.name: The Hidden Truth
cards.rules_text: Search any player's library, hand, or graveyard. You may put a permanent card found this way onto the battlefield under your control or cast a nonpermanent card found this way without paying its mana cost. If you searched a library, its owner shuffles.
cards.flavor_text: Ask anything. The answer will haunt you.

cards.name: The Idiot
cards.rules_text:
Enchant player
The Idiot has hexproof from enchanted player.
Enchanted player casts spells and activates abilities with all targets, modes, and choices made at random from the legal options.
At the beginning of enchanted player's upkeep, they mill two cards, then discard a card at random.
Enchanted player can't look at the top card of their library or any face-down cards, and loses 1 life whenever they draw a card.
cards.flavor_text: "Once-sharp thoughts now scatter like leaves in a storm, never to be gathered again."

cards.name: The Inquisitor
cards.rules_text:
Flash
When this creature enters, look at target opponent's hand; you may exile a card from it. Until your next turn ends, that player can't cast spells sharing a card type with the exiled card.
{T}, {2}, Sacrifice this creature: Exile up to three cards from target opponent's hand. This turn, you may cast one of them without paying its mana cost. At the beginning of the next end step, return the others to their owner's hand.
cards.flavor_text: "Your secrets are but bargaining chips in our little game of truth."

cards.name: The Joke
cards.rules_text:
Choose another player; that player is your ally until end of turn.
Exile the top three cards of the Deck of Many Things face up. Your ally casts one of them, then you cast one of the remaining two — each without paying its mana cost. Exile the third.
cards.flavor_text: "Three fates revealed, two embraced, one forever lost — even destiny has a sense of humor."

cards.name: The Juggler
cards.rules_text:
Flash, haste
When this creature enters, exile the top three cards of your library face up. While exiled, you may cast them, spending mana as though it were any color.
At the beginning of your upkeep, you may return any number of exiled cards you own to your library, then exile that many from the top of your library face up.
Whenever you cast a spell from exile, copy it and create a token copy of this creature. You may choose new targets for the copy.
cards.flavor_text: "Reality is merely another ball to keep in the air."

cards.name: The Keep
cards.rules_text:
When The Keep enters, draw two cards, create two Treasure tokens, then exile any number of permanents you control, each gaining "You may play this card from exile."
At the beginning of your upkeep, you may return up to two cards exiled with The Keep to the battlefield under your control.
{2}, {T}: Exile target permanent you control. It gains "You may play this card from exile."
cards.flavor_text: A fortress between worlds, where even time asks permission to enter.

cards.name: The Liar
cards.rules_text:
Flash
When The Liar enters, put a curse counter on target artifact or Equipment an opponent controls with the highest mana value.
Whenever a permanent with a curse counter is sacrificed or destroyed, you create a Treasure token and draw a card.
{T}: Put a curse counter on target artifact.
cards.flavor_text: "Truth bends like light through crystal — and I am the one who holds the prism."

cards.name: The Locksmith
cards.rules_text:
Flash
When The Locksmith enters, choose one —
• Until your next turn, target permanent loses all abilities and can't be sacrificed.
• Create a colorless Key artifact token with "{T}, Sacrifice this: Target permanent can't attack, block, or activate abilities until end of turn."
• Look at the top five cards of the Deck of Many Things and put them back in any order, then put a key counter on The Locksmith.
{1}, {T}: Put a key counter on target artifact, creature, or enchantment.
{5}, {T}, Remove all key counters from permanents you control: Gain control of target permanent with mana value X or less, where X is the number of counters removed. Activate only as a sorcery and only once per game.
cards.flavor_text: There is no door I cannot open. That is both my gift and your warning.

cards.name: The Lost
cards.rules_text:
All non-Spirit creatures lose all abilities and can't gain abilities, transform, or have counters put on them.
If a non-Spirit creature would die, exile it instead and its controller creates a 1/1 black and green Spirit token with "This creature's power and toughness are each equal to the number of Spirits you control."
Whenever a Spirit enters, its controller gains 1 life and each other player loses 1 life.
Whenever a non-Spirit creature enters, its controller loses 2 life and each other player gains 2 life.
cards.flavor_text: "Some paths, once abandoned, lead only to stranger roads."

cards.name: The Marriage
cards.rules_text:
When The Marriage enters, you and target player each gain control of target nonland permanent the other controls. Put a blessing counter on each.
At the beginning of each end step, if you control four or more permanents with blessing counters, you may remove all blessing counters from permanents you control. If you do, search your library for any number of permanent cards with total mana value X or less, where X is twice the number of counters removed, and put them onto the battlefield with blessing counters.
cards.flavor_text: The vows were spoken between worlds, and the multiverse bore witness.

cards.name: The Midwife
cards.rules_text:
Flash
When The Midwife enters, create two 1/1 white and green Spirit tokens with lifelink.
Whenever a nontoken permanent not named The Midwife enters under your control, put a birth counter on it.
At the beginning of your upkeep, you may remove a birth counter from a permanent you control to create a non-legendary token copy of it.
cards.flavor_text: Where she walks, nothing remains barren.

cards.name: The Mountain Man
cards.rules_text:
Haste, trample, ward {2}
When The Mountain Man enters or attacks, you may search your library for up to two land cards, put them onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle.
The Mountain Man's power and toughness are each equal to twice the number of lands you control, rounded down.
Landfall — This creature deals damage equal to half the number of lands you control, rounded down, to any target.
cards.flavor_text: "The peaks do not yield to those who climb them. They yield to those who become them."

cards.name: The Mute Hag
cards.rules_text:
Shroud, indestructible
When The Mute Hag enters, each opponent gets a deprivation counter of a random type.
At the beginning of each opponent's upkeep, if that player has fewer than three deprivation counters, they get one of a random type they don't already have.
Deprivation counters can't be removed except by an effect from a card named The Fates.
cards.flavor_text: "In the absence of one sense, terror fills all others."

cards.name: The Owl
cards.rules_text:
Flash
Flying, ward {2}
Whenever you scry, gain 2 life and draw a card.
At the beginning of your upkeep, scry 2.
{T}, Sacrifice The Owl: Scry 7, then look at the top three cards of each opponent's library. You may put any of them on the bottom of their owners' libraries. Then target opponent may scry 3. You draw three cards.
When The Owl dies, investigate.
cards.flavor_text: It sees what you see. It sees what they see. It sees what no one sees.

cards.name: The Paladin
cards.rules_text:
Flying, double strike, vigilance
When The Paladin enters, choose one —
• Create five 2/2 white Knight tokens with vigilance.
• Gain 10 life and draw three cards.
• Get an emblem with "Creatures you control have protection from the color of your choice and lifelink."
Whenever The Paladin attacks, exile target nonland permanent an opponent controls until The Paladin leaves.
At the beginning of your end step, return target creature card with mana value 6 or less from your graveyard to the battlefield.
cards.flavor_text: She arrived without being summoned. Evil had that effect on her.

cards.name: The Peacock
cards.rules_text:
When The Peacock enters, creatures you control gain indestructible until end of turn.
Creatures you control get -1/+4 and have hexproof.
Creatures can't attack you unless their controller pays {2} for each creature they control that's attacking you.
cards.flavor_text: Invulnerable, immaculate, immovable.

cards.name: The Publican
cards.rules_text:
When The Publican enters, create a Tavern artifact token.
Whenever a player creates a Patron token, each player may pay {1}. Each player who does creates a Treasure token and gains 1 life.
At the beginning of each end step, any player who controls three or more Patron tokens may exile those tokens. When they do, that player gains control of target nonland permanent and creates a Tavern token.
cards.flavor_text: "A round of drinks opens more doors than a battering ram."

cards.name: The Queen Mother
cards.rules_text:
When this creature enters, create X 1/2 green and black Ant tokens with reach and vigilance, where X is two plus 2d4.
This creature has indestructible and hexproof while you control an Ant.
Ants you control have "Whenever this creature blocks or becomes blocked, put a +1/+1 counter on it."
Whenever this creature becomes the target of an opponent's spell or ability, create a 2/3 green and black Ant token with reach and vigilance.
cards.flavor_text: She speaks, and ten thousand legs answer.

cards.name: The Rabbit Prince
cards.rules_text:
Double strike, haste
If a creature you control would deal combat damage, it deals double that damage instead.
Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player for the first time each turn, you may have target creature you control gain double strike and haste and get +X/+0 until end of turn, where X is the combat damage this creature dealt that player this turn. Untap all creatures you control. After this phase, there is an additional combat phase.
cards.flavor_text: "In the Court of Swift Justice, every strike tells two tales."— The Rabbit Prince

cards.name: The Rakshasa
cards.flavor_text: Every deception carries a price. The Rakshasa simply collects.

cards.name: Curse of Leprosy
cards.rules_text:
Enchant player
When this Curse enters and at the beginning of your upkeep, put a disease counter on it.
At the beginning of your end step, lose 2 life for each disease counter on it.
Creatures you control can't gain counters and get -1/-1 for each disease counter on it.
Pay life equal to twice the number of disease counters on it: Remove a disease counter. Activate only as a sorcery and only while it has two or more.
cards.flavor_text: "The flesh remembers what the mind wishes to forget."

cards.name: The Snakebite
cards.rules_text:
Split second
Put X -1/-1 counters on target creature, where X is twice that creature's power. Then create X 1/1 green Snake tokens with deathtouch and wither.
If this spell would be put into a graveyard from the stack, instead exile it with three time counters. Suspend
When you cast this card from exile, double the number of poison counters each player has.
cards.flavor_text: "First comes fear. Then pain. Finally, the blessed numbness of acceptance."

cards.name: The Survivor
cards.rules_text: If you would lose the game, instead shuffle your hand, your graveyard, and all permanents you own into your library, then draw seven cards and your life total becomes your starting life total. You may cast three spells from your hand without paying their mana costs.
cards.flavor_text: She died. And then she didn't.

cards.name: The Tangled Briar
cards.rules_text:
{T}: Add one mana of any color.
{T}: Look at the top X cards of your library, where X is the number of lands you control. You may put a land card from among them onto the battlefield. If you do, you may cast a creature spell from your hand without paying its mana cost. Then roll a d6 and a random opponent creates that many plus two 3/3 green Plant tokens with trample that must attack you each combat if able.
Landfall — Whenever a land you control enters, put a +1/+1 counter on target creature you control.
cards.flavor_text: The briar whispers where treasure lies buried. The shambling mounds remember where you dug.

cards.name: The Teamster
cards.rules_text:
When The Teamster enters, create a Quest enchantment token with "At the beginning of your upkeep, you lose 2 life, then put a time counter on it," "When you cast a spell with mana value 5 or greater, sacrifice this enchantment, search your library for a card, put it into your hand, shuffle, and draw two cards," and "When this has five or more time counters, sacrifice it and you get an emblem with 'At the beginning of your upkeep, you lose 2 life.'"
{1}, Sacrifice The Teamster: Create a 4/4 black and white Avatar token with flying and lifelink and two Treasure tokens. Activate only if you don't control a Quest token or have an emblem from The Teamster.
cards.flavor_text: The task was impossible. The payment was irresistible.

cards.name: The Theatre
cards.rules_text:
At the beginning of your upkeep, create a 1/1 green and white Human Performer token and put a Spotlight counter on this artifact.
Whenever a creature dies, you may put a Spotlight counter on it.
{T}, Remove a Spotlight counter: Choose one —
• Encore performance — Create two 2/2 white Human Performer tokens with "When this creature enters, tap target creature an opponent controls."
• Inspire the audience — Each player draws a card, then you draw two additional cards.
• Standing ovation — Each player gains 1 life, then you gain 1 life for each creature you control.
cards.flavor_text: The curtain rises. The audience holds its breath. You are both actor and playwright.

cards.name: The Trumpet
cards.rules_text:
Activated abilities of permanents you control cost {1} less to activate for each two lands you control. This can't reduce a cost below {1}.
{8}, {T}: Create a creature token with power and toughness each equal to half the number of lands you control, rounded down. Choose one —
• A white Knight with first strike and vigilance.
• A black Demon with menace and deathtouch.
• A blue Elemental with flying and lifelink.
{8}, {T}: Search your library for a creature card with mana value less than or equal to half the number of lands you control, rounded down. Reveal it, put it into your hand, then shuffle. You may cast it this turn without paying its mana cost.
cards.flavor_text: One note. Across every plane, every hero turns toward the sound.

cards.name: The Twin
cards.rules_text:
Flash, haste
{T}: This creature becomes a copy of target creature, except its power and toughness are swapped and +1/+1 counters on it become -1/-1 counters. It gains this ability, and that creature becomes twinned with this creature.
If this creature or a creature twinned with it would leave the battlefield, instead both are put into their owners' graveyards.
cards.flavor_text: It wore his face, but everything behind the eyes was wrong.

cards.name: The Tyrant
cards.rules_text:
Vigilance, menace, indestructible
When this creature enters, you become the monarch.
As long as you're the monarch:
• At the beginning of your upkeep, each opponent sacrifices a permanent. For each permanent sacrificed this way, create a Treasure token and put a +1/+1 counter on this creature.
• Whenever you cast a spell, copy it. You may choose new targets for the copy.
• Other players can't cast spells during your turn, and opponents can't attack you with fewer than three creatures.
cards.flavor_text: "Every crown casts two shadows: one of glory above, one of graves below."

cards.name: The Unicorn
cards.rules_text:
Hexproof, indestructible
{T}, Shuffle this creature into the Deck of Many Things: Choose one —
• Rewrite the Past — Return all creatures put into your graveyard this turn to the battlefield.
• Shape the Future — Shuffle up to five cards from your graveyard into your library, then draw two cards.
• Unravel Time — Look at the top three cards of the Deck of Many Things. Exile one, shuffle one into the Deck, and put the last on top.
• Divine Undoing — Return to the battlefield under your control a permanent card you own that left the battlefield this game.
cards.flavor_text: "In its radiant presence, regret dissolves and time itself bows, offering a second chance to those who dare to dream anew."

cards.name: The Uprising
cards.rules_text:
When this enchantment enters, roll 3d6. Create that many 1/1 white Human Peasant tokens.
Accident-Prone — At the beginning of each combat, if you control three or more Peasants, one of them chosen at random must attack if able.
Reputation — Whenever a Peasant you control dies, each opponent draws a card.
{2}: Peasants you control get +2/+0 and gain haste until end of turn. Activate only as a sorcery.
cards.flavor_text: "Their loyalty was pure. Their competence... less so."

cards.name: The Vision
cards.rules_text:
An opponent chosen at random secretly chooses one of these omens. An omen takes effect at the beginning of your next turn:
• Peace — Draw five cards, gain 10 life, and you may put a land card from your hand onto the battlefield.
• War — Destroy target creature each opponent controls. Create three 3/3 black Knight tokens with menace.
At the beginning of your next upkeep, choose one —
• Embrace — The chosen omen occurs.
• Reject — The unchosen omen occurs.
cards.flavor_text: "Two futures dance before your eyes. One true, one false... but which is which?"

cards.name: The Wanderer
cards.rules_text:
When this enchantment enters, choose target nonland permanent you control. It gains indestructible and hexproof, becomes legendary, and gains one of the following at random —
• Manaforge — "{T}: Add three mana of any one color."
• Insight — "{T}: Draw two cards, then discard a card."
• Replication — "{T}, Pay 3 life: Create a token copy of this permanent."
• Rebirth — "{T}, Sacrifice this permanent: Return any number of permanent cards from your graveyard to the battlefield."
cards.flavor_text: She touched the iron pan, and it sang with the voice of a god.

cards.name: The Waxworks
cards.rules_text: Roll a d6. An opponent chosen at random creates that many 3/3 blue, black, and red Shapeshifter tokens. Each can't be blocked by creatures that share a color with it, must attack the player who drew The Waxworks each combat if able, has all activated abilities of creatures that player controls, and deals 2 damage to that player when it dies.
cards.flavor_text: "In every reflection lies a darker self, yearning to destroy its origin."

cards.name: The Winged Serpent
cards.rules_text:
Flying, hexproof, haste
{1}: Look at the top three cards of your library, then put them back in any order.
{T}: Draw a card.
Sovereign Wish — Sacrifice this creature: Choose one —
• Search your library for a card and put it onto the battlefield. If it's a creature, it gains haste.
• Exile all creatures and planeswalkers your opponents control.
• Gain control of all nonland permanents target opponent controls.
• Target player's life total becomes 1 or 40.
• Target player skips their next turn, or you take an extra turn after this one.
• Return any number of permanent cards from your graveyard to the battlefield.
• Remove the Deck of Many Things from the game. Each player shuffles their hand, graveyard, and all permanents they own into their library, then draws seven cards. You may put any number of permanents from your hand onto the battlefield.
cards.flavor_text: Name your heart's desire. It has already been granted.

cards.name: Plane of Air
cards.rules_text:
At the beginning of your upkeep, you lose 5 life unless you control a creature with flying.
You can't play lands.
Each non-flying creature you control becomes a 1/1 and can't block.
Whenever you cast a spell, roll a d20:
1-5: Counter that spell.
6-10: Counter that spell unless you pay {2}.
11-15: Copy that spell. You may choose new targets for the copy.
16-20: Copy that spell twice. You may choose new targets for the copies.

cards.name: The Beginning
cards.rules_text:
I — Each player shuffles their hand, graveyard, cards they own in exile, and permanents they control into their library, draws seven, gains 10 life.
II — Search your library for two lands, put them onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle.
III — You get an emblem with "At the beginning of your upkeep, scry 1, then draw a card. You have no maximum hand size."
IV — Take an extra turn after this one. During it, you may play an additional land and spells you cast cost {2} less.

cards.name: The Champion
cards.rules_text:
First strike, vigilance
When this creature enters, put two shield counters on it and create a 2/2 white Knight token with vigilance for each opponent.
At the beginning of combat on your turn, put a shield counter on this creature.
This creature gets +1/+1 for each shield counter on it, and other creatures you control get +X/+0 where X is that number.
If this creature would be dealt lethal damage, instead remove all shield counters from it, untap it, and exile target nonland permanent an opponent controls.
cards.flavor_text: "With each battle won, another hero rises to follow. Such is the way of legends." —Elspeth Tirel

cards.name: The Chancellor
cards.rules_text:
Flash
When this creature enters, scry 3.
At the beginning of your upkeep, look at the top three cards of target player's library and reorder them. If it was an opponent's, draw a card.
{1}, {T}: You may copy target spell and choose new targets for the copy. Its controller reveals the top card of their library. Counter that spell unless they pay {X} equal to that card's mana value.
cards.flavor_text: "I do not predict the future. I arrange it." -- Archmage Remora

cards.name: The Chaos
cards.rules_text: When The Chaos enters and at the beginning of your upkeep, roll a six-sided die. Until your next upkeep, you and each creature you control gain protection based on the result: 1 red and white, 2 blue and red, 3 black and blue, 4 green and black, 5 white and green, 6 all colors and colorless.
cards.flavor_text: "Chaos does not choose sides. It merely chooses, and we must adapt to its whims." —Zariel, Archduke of Avernus

cards.name: The Coin
cards.rules_text:
When The Coin enters, create five Gold tokens.
At the beginning of your upkeep, create a Gold token.
Sacrifice The Coin: Choose one, where X is the number of artifacts you control —
• Create X Treasure tokens.
• Draw X cards.
• Flip X coins. For each flip you win, target opponent loses 3 life and you gain 3 life.
cards.flavor_text: "Fortune doesn't favor the bold. Fortune favors whoever holds me." -- The Coin

cards.name: The Crown
cards.rules_text:
When The Crown enters, you become the monarch. Each opponent chooses a creature they control with power 4 or less. Each chosen creature attacks a player or planeswalker of your choice other than you this turn if able.
Creatures you control with power 4 or less have menace.
{T}: Gain control of target creature with power 4 or less until end of turn, untap it, and it gains haste.
cards.flavor_text: "It whispered to her: 'Wear me, and the small shall kneel.'" -- The Crown of Friends

cards.name: The Dawn
cards.rules_text:
When The Dawn enters, exile all creatures, then return them to the battlefield under their owners' control. Untap all lands. Each player creates two 1/1 white Human tokens and gains 3 life.
Creatures have vigilance and lifelink.
At the beginning of each player's upkeep, that player may put a +1/+1 counter on target creature they control.
cards.flavor_text: "As light banishes shadow, so too does hope restore the broken."

cards.name: The Day
cards.rules_text:
When The Day enters, each player creates two 1/1 white Soldier tokens with vigilance and gains 3 life.
Creatures have first strike. Damage dealt by creatures can't be prevented.
At the beginning of each player's upkeep, that player may have target creature they control gain double strike until end of turn.
cards.flavor_text: "At the height of the sun's power, blades never dull, and warriors never tire."

cards.name: The Destiny
cards.rules_text: Create an emblem with "The next time you would lose the game, instead your life total becomes 1, then exile this emblem."
cards.flavor_text: "Not today." -- Serra's Knight, sole survivor of the Fallen Gates

cards.name: The Dusk
cards.rules_text:
When The Dusk enters, exile all creatures, then return them tapped under their owners' control. Each player creates two 1/1 black Spirit tokens with flying and loses 3 life.
Creatures have menace and lifelink.
At the beginning of each player's upkeep, that player may put a -1/-1 counter on target creature an opponent controls.
cards.flavor_text: "As the sun dips below the horizon, shadows lengthen, and the world grows weary."

cards.name: Omen of Death
cards.rules_text:
When Omen of Death enters, you lose 7 life.
Your life total can't become greater than 25.
You can't gain life.
Harbinger — Whenever a creature deals combat damage to you, you lose the game.
At the beginning of your third upkeep after Omen of Death entered the battlefield, exile it.
cards.flavor_text: "The hourglass empties. The hand reaches. The last breath is always yours to take."

cards.name: The Isolation
cards.rules_text:
Split second
Exile all permanents you control and all cards in your hand. At the beginning of your next upkeep, return all cards exiled this way to the battlefield and to your hand. Until your next turn, you have hexproof and can't be attacked.
cards.flavor_text: "Gone for four minutes. Aged four years." -- Journal of the Lost Wanderer

cards.name: The Justice
cards.rules_text:
Players can't cast more than one spell each turn.
If a player would gain or lose life, instead each player gains or loses that much life.
Whenever a player would search their library for a card, instead each player may search their library for a card of that type, then each player shuffles.
cards.flavor_text: "What touches one, touches all." -- The Scales That Never Tip

cards.name: The Knife
cards.rules_text:
Living weapon
Equipped creature gets +4/+4, has haste, indestructible, and double strike.
Whenever equipped creature deals combat damage to a player, you may exile target nonland permanent that player controls. If you do, that player creates a Treasure token.
Equip {3}
cards.flavor_text: "It chose me. I wish it hadn't." -- The Knife's last wielder

cards.name: The Lock
cards.rules_text:
Activated abilities of permanents your opponents control cost {1} more.
{T}: Target permanent loses all abilities until end of turn. If it was an artifact, create a Treasure token.
{1}, {T}: Counter target activated or triggered ability. If its source is an artifact, gain control of it instead.
{T}, Sacrifice The Lock: Each opponent reveals their hand. Exile any number of those nonland cards face-down until your next end step.
cards.flavor_text: "Some things are locked away to protect us. Some things are locked away to protect themselves." -- Master Artificer Jin

cards.name: The Mischief
cards.rules_text:
Choose one —
• Search your library for an artifact card with mana value 3 or less, put it onto the battlefield, then shuffle your library.
• Draw two additional cards from the Deck of Many Things.
cards.flavor_text: Two doors. One safe, one not. Guess which one she picked.

sim_effect.label: The Mischief — draw up to 2 more from the Deck?

cards.name: The Monster
cards.rules_text:
Enchant player
Reveal the first card you draw each turn.
Whenever you cast a spell, mill cards equal to its mana value. If any creature cards were milled, counter that spell unless you pay {1} for each of them.
At the beginning of your end step, lose 2 life for each spell The Monster countered this turn.
cards.flavor_text: "Look upon me, mortal. Let my visage burn itself into your dreams, until every shadow bears my face and every mirror holds my gaze." — The Monster

cards.name: The Mystery
cards.rules_text:
When The Mystery enters, each player exiles the top three cards of their library face down.
At the beginning of each player's upkeep, that player may exile the top card of their library face down, then may cast a random card exiled with The Mystery without paying its mana cost. If they do, they lose life equal to the number of cards they own exiled with The Mystery.
When The Mystery leaves, shuffle all cards exiled with it into their owners' libraries.
cards.flavor_text: The answer was there, hidden among the lies. But which card held the truth?

cards.name: The Night
cards.rules_text:
When The Night enters, each player exiles the top four cards of their library face-down, creates two 1/1 black Bat tokens with flying, and loses 3 life.
Creatures have menace. Players may look at the top card of each library at any time.
At the beginning of each player's upkeep, that player may scry 2, then put a card from their hand on the bottom of their library.
cards.flavor_text: "In the stillness of night, secrets are whispered, and the unseen stirs."

cards.name: The Order
cards.rules_text:
When The Order enters, choose a color.
If a source of the chosen color would deal damage to you or a creature you control, prevent it. Whenever damage is prevented this way, target creature you control gets +X/+X until end of turn, where X is that damage.
Spells of the chosen color your opponents cast cost {2} more.
At the beginning of your upkeep, you may change the color.
cards.flavor_text: Let them cast their spells. None of that color shall touch us.

cards.name: The Student
cards.rules_text:
When The Student enters, look at the top three cards of your library. You may put a land card from among them into your hand. Put the rest on the bottom.
Whenever you would draw a card during your draw step, instead scry 1, then draw a card.
At the beginning of your end step, draw a card.
cards.flavor_text: She didn't learn magic. She learned how to learn. That was enough.

cards.name: The Vulture
cards.rules_text:
Flying, haste
When this creature enters or attacks, exile target artifact an opponent controls. At the beginning of that player's next upkeep, they may pay {3}. If they do, return it to the battlefield under their control. If they don't, at the beginning of that turn's end step, put it into its owner's graveyard and create a Treasure token.
cards.flavor_text: It always knows which trinket you love most.
